rulings.Case Title: CONSUELO LOPEZ et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, OCTOBER 25, 2007, LINE 3, DEFENDANT J.T. THORPE & SON, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN ON CAUSATION BY RELYING ON PLAINTIFFS ORIGINAL ANSWERS TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES CANNOT BE RELIED UPON BECAUSE: 1)THEY ARE UNVERIFIED; 2)THEY ARE HEARSAY AND 3)THE RESPONDING PARTY MAY NOT RELY ON ITS OWN RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES. CCP SECTION 2030.410. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. (a) DEFENDANT IS NOT LIABLE FOR STRICT LIABILITY IN TORT AS NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT MANUFACTURED OR "SUPPLIED" AN ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCT. (b)THERE IS NO EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THE FALSE REPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION. (c)THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO SUPPORT THE PUNITIVE DAMAGE CLAIM. (d)THE LOSS OF CONSORTIUM CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE IT IS DERIVATIVE. =(302/JKS/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CASUALTY INSURANCE CO VS. WOLDEMARIM GHEBREYESUS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, OCTOBER 25, 2007, LINE 6, DEFENDANT WOLDEMARIAM GHEBGREYESUS MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S PROOF OF SERVICE SHOWS PERSONAL SERVICE ON DEFENDANT ON FEBRUARY 26, 2006 AT 706 VIENNA STREET. DEFENDANT CLAIMS HE WAS NOT LIVING AT 706 VIENNA STREET AS OF MARCH 6, 2006. DEFENDANT ALSO FAILED TO FILE A RESPONSIVE PLEADING AS REQUIRED BY CCP SECTION 473.5. =(302/JKS/AY)

rulings.Case Title: LUKE O'BRIEN et al VS. GREGORY RESNICK et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, OCTOBER 25, 2007, LINE 10, DEFENDANTS GREGORY RESNICK AND PEARL RESNICK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE FOLLOWING FACTS WERE DISCLOSED TO THE BUYERS BEFORE CONTRACT FORMATION AND WHETHER THESE FACTS WERE MATERIAL TO THE BUYERS DECISION TO PURCHASE THE PROPERTY! 1. THE 3/29/05 OMI NOTICE FOR TERMINATION OF TENANCY, WHICH INCLUDED AN OFFER TO TENANT RUBIN THAT SHE COULD MOVE INTO THE COTTAGE (1006A) FOR $1,100/MONTH RENT; 2. NOTICE OF CONSTRAINTS, DATED 6/10/05; 3. TWO LETTERS, DATED 12/04, THAT HAD BEEN DISCLOSED TO THE RESNICKS FROM TENANT RUBIN'S ATTORNEY STATING THAT RUBIN WAS DISABLED AND PROTECTED UNDER THE SF RENT ORDINANCE AND DEMANDING $24,000 AS A BUYOUT PRICE. THIS DISPUTE AROSE OUT OF THE PREVIOUS OWNER'S ATTEMPTED EVICTION OF MS. RUBIN WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE GROUNDS UNDER THE SAN FRANCISCO RENT ORDINANCE; 4. 9/26/05 COMPLAINT FOR UNLAWFUL DETAINER BASED ON OMI NOTICE FILED BY MR. RESNICK AND VERIFIED, BY MR. RESNICK UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY; 5. 10/24/05 CORRESPONDENCE FROM MR. RESNICK'S ATTORNEY TO LISTING AGENT KIRKPATRICK STATING THAT HE WANTED TO CANCEL THE LISTING AND CONCLUDE THE LANDLORD-TENANT LITIGATION MATTER BEFORE ANY SALE; 6. 11/1/05 LETTER FROM MR. MARGUILES, ONE DAY BEFORE 11/2/05 OFFER CONFIRMIMG PLAN AND UNDERSTANDING THAT TENANT RUBIN WOULD MOVE INTO THE COTTAGE AS $895/MONTH RENT; 7. THAT LISTING AGENT MR. KIRKPATRICK OBSERVED TENANT RUBIN, CONCLUDING THAT SHE HAD PSYCHOLOGICAL PROBLEMS, WAS ANTISOCIAL, AND A PACKRAT WITH A TON OF PERSONAL BELONGINGS ALL OVER THE HOUSE; 8. THAT NO RENTS WERE COLLECTED FROM TENANT RUBIN SINCE ON OR ABOUT LATE MAY 2005 WHEN THE EVICTION NOTICE EXPIRED; 9. THAT DEFENDANT RESNICK HAD SPENT ALMOST A YEAR ATTEMPTING TO EVICT TENANT RUBIN AND THAT RUBIN HAD PROMISED TO MOVE FOR MONEY BUT CHANGED HER MIND SEVERAL TIMES. IT IS NOT NECESSARY THAT PLAINTIFFS ESTABLISH CONTINUING RELIANCE FROM THE TIME THE INITIAL

rulings.Case Title: NEVEO MOSSER VS. MICHAEL MURAWSKI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, OCTOBER 25, 2007, LINE 14, DEFENDANTS MICHAEL MURAWSKI AND KIMBERLY MURAWSKI'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS CLAIM FOR MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE OR SURPRISE IS MERITORIOUS IN THAT DEFENDANTS WERE ATTEMPTING TO SETTLE THE MATTER BY PAYING RENT AND DID NOT REALIZE THAT A DEFAULT WOULD BE TAKEN. =(302/JKS/AY)
